key_insight: Scratch testing is a fast, sensitive way to assess adhesion and cohesive failure in thermally sprayed coatings, but its results are only meaningful when linked to coating microstructure, residual stress, porosity, and failure mode.
engineering_relevance: For aerospace, energy, automotive, and industrial coatings, the review shows how scratch-test interpretation can guide coating selection, process optimization, and reliability screening.
method: Compared scratch response across thermal spray routes such as APS, HVOF, HVAF, and D-gun.
Correlated critical load and failure evolution with microstructure, porosity, and residual stress.
Reviewed critical-load evaluation tools including microscopy, acoustic emission, and multi-axis force sensing.
evidence_signal: The paper synthesizes and compares a broad set of published coating systems and scratch-test methodologies rather than presenting new experimental data.
watch_item: Scratch-test outcomes are highly method-dependent, so inconsistent test setups and failure criteria can lead to non-comparable critical loads.
paper_title: A critical analysis of the scratch test for thermally sprayed coatings
